affected.product: All in One SEO – Powerful SEO Plugin to Boost SEO Rankings & Increase Traffic

descriptions.value: The All in One SEO – Powerful SEO Plugin to Boost SEO Rankings & Increase Traffic plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via the post Meta Description and Canonical URL parameters in all versions up to, and including, 4.8.1.1 due to insufficient input sanitization and output escaping. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with Contributor-level access and above, to inject arbitrary web scripts in pages that will execute whenever a user accesses an injected page.

cna.title: All in One SEO Pack <= 4.8.1.1 - Authenticated (Contributor+) Stored Cross-Site Scripting via Post Meta Description and Canonical URL

descriptions.description: CWE-79 Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

cna.title: All in One SEO < 4.6.1.1 - Contributor+ Stored XSS

descriptions.value: The All in One SEO  WordPress plugin before 4.6.1.1 does not validate and escape some of its Post fields before outputting them back, which could allow users with the contributor role and above to perform Stored Cross-Site Scripting attacks

affected.product: All in One SEO – Powerful SEO Plugin to Boost SEO Rankings & Increase Traffic

descriptions.value: The All in One SEO Pack plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via multiple parameters in versions up to, and including, 4.2.9 due to insufficient input sanitization and output escaping. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers with Administrator role or above to inject arbitrary web scripts in pages that will execute whenever a user accesses an injected page.

cna.title: All in One SEO Pack <= 4.2.9 - Authenticated (Administrator+) Stored Cross-Site Scripting

affected.product: All in One SEO – Best WordPress SEO Plugin – Easily Improve SEO Rankings & Increase Traffic

descriptions.value: The All in One SEO WordPress plugin before 4.1.5.3 is affected by an authenticated SQL injection issue, which was discovered during an internal audit by the Jetpack Scan team, and could grant attackers access to privileged information from the affected site’s database (e.g., usernames and hashed passwords).

cna.title: All In One SEO < 4.1.5.3 - Authenticated SQL Injection

CVE_data_meta.TITLE: All In One SEO < 4.1.5.3 - Authenticated SQL Injection

product_data.product_name: All in One SEO – Best WordPress SEO Plugin – Easily Improve SEO Rankings & Increase Traffic

description_data.value: The All in One SEO WordPress plugin before 4.1.5.3 is affected by an authenticated SQL injection issue, which was discovered during an internal audit by the Jetpack Scan team, and could grant attackers access to privileged information from the affected site’s database (e.g., usernames and hashed passwords).

affected.product: All in One SEO – Best WordPress SEO Plugin – Easily Improve SEO Rankings & Increase Traffic

descriptions.value: The All in One SEO WordPress plugin before 4.1.5.3 is affected by a Privilege Escalation issue, which was discovered during an internal audit by the Jetpack Scan team, and may grant bad actors access to protected REST API endpoints they shouldn’t have access to. This could ultimately enable users with low-privileged accounts, like subscribers, to perform remote code execution on affected sites.

cna.title: All In One SEO < 4.1.5.3 - Authenticated Privilege Escalation

CVE_data_meta.TITLE: All In One SEO < 4.1.5.3 - Authenticated Privilege Escalation

This could ultimately enable users with low-privileged accounts, like subscribers, to perform remote code execution on affected sites.

affected.product: All in One SEO – Best WordPress SEO Plugin – Easily Improve Your SEO Rankings

descriptions.value: The All in One SEO – Best WordPress SEO Plugin – Easily Improve Your SEO Rankings before 4.1.0.2 enables authenticated users with "aioseo_tools_settings" privilege (most of the time admin) to execute arbitrary code on the underlying host. Users can restore plugin's configuration by uploading a backup .ini file in the section "Tool > Import/Export". However, the plugin attempts to unserialize values of the .ini file. Moreover, the plugin embeds Monolog library which can be used to craft a gadget chain and thus trigger system command execution.

cna.title: All in One SEO Pack <  4.1.0.2 - Admin RCE via unserialize

CVE_data_meta.TITLE: All in One SEO Pack <  4.1.0.2 - Admin RCE via unserialize

product_data.product_name: All in One SEO – Best WordPress SEO Plugin – Easily Improve Your SEO Rankings

description_data.value: The All in One SEO – Best WordPress SEO Plugin – Easily Improve Your SEO Rankings before 4.1.0.2 enables authenticated users with "aioseo_tools_settings" privilege (most of the time admin) to execute arbitrary code on the underlying host. Users can restore plugin's configuration by uploading a backup .ini file in the section "Tool > Import/Export". However, the plugin attempts to unserialize values of the .ini file. Moreover, the plugin embeds Monolog library which can be used to craft a gadget chain and thus trigger system command execution.

affected.product: All in One SEO – Powerful SEO Plugin to Boost SEO Rankings & Increase Traffic

descriptions.value: The All in One SEO – Best WordPress SEO Plugin – Easily Improve SEO Rankings & Increase Traffic plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via the plugin's shortcode(s) in all versions up to, and including, 4.6.0 due to insufficient input sanitization and output escaping on user supplied attributes. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with contributor-level access and above, to inject arbitrary web scripts in pages that will execute whenever a user accesses an injected page.

cna.title: All in One SEO – Best WordPress SEO Plugin – Easily Improve SEO Rankings & Increase Traffic <= 4.6.0 - Authenticated (Contributor+) Stored Cross-Site Scripting via Shortcode
